title: Grep errors in syslog
description: Search lines containing "ERROR" in syslog.

title: List open ports (macOS)
description: Show listening TCP ports (no DNS).

title: Kill process by name
description: Send SIGTERM to processes matching name.

title: Tail file with colors
description: Follow log with colorized output using ccze.

title: Find large files
description: Find the 20 largest files/directories.

title: Check disk usage (human)
description: Mounted disks with human units.

title: Show git branch graph
description: Compact commit graph.

title: Copy public SSH key
description: Copy your public key to clipboard (macOS).

title: Check Python package versions
description: List outdated installed packages.

title: Rsync local → local (human, progress)
description: Archive mode, human-readable, with progress. Trailing slash copies contents of SRC/.

title: Rsync over SSH (resume + compress)
description: Archive + compress, with partial/resume and progress. Uses SSH.

title: Mirror with delete (dangerous)
description: Make DEST a mirror of SRC (delete extraneous files).

title: Rsync with checksum compare
description: Verify by checksums instead of modtime/size (slower).

title: Rsync exclude patterns
description: Skip matching paths with --exclude; repeat for multiple patterns.

title: Rsync limit bandwidth (over SSH)
description: Throttle transfer to ~2 MB/s to avoid saturating a link.

title: Rsync 'mrchivas' mnemonic
description: Mnemonic: -m prune-empty, -r recursive, -c checksum (slower), -h human, -i itemize, -v verbose, -a archive, -s safe. Note: -a already implies -r.

title: Serve current folder (8000)
description: Quick static file server.

title: Watch unit logs (last 1h)
description: Systemd logs for a unit since the last hour, then follow.

title: xargs parallel (4 jobs)
description: Run up to 4 gzip jobs in parallel over found files.

title: strace a PID (network only)
description: Attach to a process and trace only network syscalls (Linux).

title: ltrace a PID (libs)
description: Trace library calls for a running process (Linux).

title: sed show first 200 lines
description: Print a line range without paging.

title: dmesg recent (human time)
description: Kernel messages with human timestamps (last 50).

title: journalctl grep last 2h (follow)
description: Filter systemd journal by regex since 2 hours ago and follow.

title: systemctl show path + desc
description: Inspect unit file location and description.

title: ss filter: established to 443
description: List established TCP connections to destination port 443.

title: sysctl read a key
description: Print current kernel param value (read-only).

title: docker compose logs (follow)
description: Follow logs for a specific Compose service.

title: nginx test & reload
command: sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
description: Validate NGINX config then reload if ok.

title: curl verbose HEAD (HTTP/2)
description: Fetch response headers with verbose TLS/HTTP/2 debug.

title: git worktree (checkout branch elsewhere)
description: Check out a branch into a sibling directory via worktree.

title: kubectl logs deployment (follow)
description: Follow last 200 lines of logs from a deployment.

title: kubectl port-forward service
description: Forward localhost:8080 to service port 80.

title: kubectl rollout status
description: Watch the rollout of a deployment.

title: Extract tar archive (auto)
description: GNU tar and bsdtar auto-detect compression; no -z needed. Add v for progress: `tar xvf file.tar.gz`.

title: List tar contents
description: Preview what’s inside without extracting. Add v for details: `tar tvf file.tar.gz`.

title: Create gzipped tar
description: Create a .tar.gz from a directory. (GNU tar: `tar caf out.tar.xz dir/` auto-picks compression by suffix.)

title: Tail live Docker logs
description: Tail live logs from a running container (like tail -f for Docker). Use -t for timestamps if you need them. Great for 2AM debugging without SSHing in.

title: List containers in table format
description: List all containers (running/stopped) in a clean table format. Add --filter "status=exited" to focus on crashed ones for cleanup.

title: Build Docker image (no cache)
description: Build an image from Dockerfile in the current dir, no cache for fresh deps, plain output for easy reading. Bump to :v1.2 for versioning.

title: Docker Compose with scaling
description: Spin up services in detached mode, rebuild if changed, scale replicas (e.g., 3 web instances). Add --force-recreate to nuke and restart everything.

title: List failed services
description: List only failed services for quick triage. Pair with journalctl -u failed.service to dive into logs—saves hunting through full status outputs.

title: Mask/unmask service
description: Temporarily disable a service (mask prevents enabling/starting); unmask to restore. Useful for safe testing without full removal—e.g., mask a buggy cron job.

title: Edit service config inline
description: Override a service's config inline (creates a drop-in file). E.g., add [Service] Environment=DEBUG=true—reload with systemctl daemon-reload after.

title: Filter service logs by error level
description: Filter error-level logs for a service since yesterday (@yesterday is a relative time spec). Add -o json for parseable output in scripts.

title: Show service properties
description: Show specific properties (load/active state) for a service. Pipe to grep for automation: `systemctl show nginx -p ActiveState | grep Active`.

title: Unit logs (last hour)
description: Show logs for a systemd unit since the last hour.

flags.desc: Show only error level and above

title: Who's listening (Linux)
description: List listening TCP/UDP sockets with owning PIDs.

title: Top dirs by size (root)
description: Disk triage without crossing filesystems (-x).

title: Processes by memory
description: Quick memory hogs overview.

flags.desc: All processes, user format, include no-tty

title: List tar contents
description: Preview archive without extracting (add v for details).

title: Create gzipped tar
description: Make a .tar.gz from a directory.

title: Docker logs (tail 10m)
description: Follow container logs since 10 minutes ago.

title: Docker ps (nice table)
description: Readable container list.

title: Docker compose up (detached)
description: Spin services in the background.

flags.desc: Recreate containers even if config unchanged
Remove containers for services not in compose file

title: Quick static server (Web)
description: Serve a build directory locally.

title: Check site headers
description: Fetch response headers only.

title: TLS expiry (dates only)
command: openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 -servername example.com -showcerts </dev/null 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates
description: See cert notBefore/notAfter for a host.

flags.desc: Connect to specific host and port

title: Interactive shell in container
description: Open an interactive bash shell inside a running container for debugging and exploration.

title: Build Docker image
description: Build a Docker image from Dockerfile in current directory with a tag.

flags.desc: Tag the image with name and version

title: List Docker networks
description: Show all Docker networks and their drivers.

title: Create Docker network
description: Create a custom Docker network for container communication.

title: List Docker volumes
description: Show all Docker volumes for persistent data storage.

title: Create Docker volume
description: Create a named volume for persistent container data.

title: Docker system cleanup
description: Remove unused containers, networks, images, and build cache to free space.

flags.desc: Remove all unused images, not just dangling

title: List pods with details
description: Show pods with additional details like node placement and IP addresses.

title: Describe Kubernetes pod
description: Get detailed information about a pod including events and configuration.

title: Follow pod logs
description: Stream live logs from a Kubernetes pod for real-time monitoring.

flags.desc: Show logs from previous container instance

title: Execute in Kubernetes pod
description: Open interactive shell in a Kubernetes pod for debugging.

flags.desc: Separator between kubectl and container command

title: Git log with graph
description: Visual commit history with branch structure in compact format.

flags.desc: Show ASCII graph of branch structure

title: Git hard reset
description: Permanently undo last commit and all changes (destructive operation).

title: Git stash with message
description: Save current changes with descriptive message for later retrieval.

title: Git cherry-pick commit
description: Apply specific commit from another branch to current branch.

title: Connect to MySQL
description: Connect to MySQL database with username, password prompt, and specific database.

title: Connect to PostgreSQL
description: Connect to PostgreSQL database with specific user, database, and host.

title: Redis monitor commands
description: Watch all Redis commands in real-time for debugging and monitoring.

title: AWS S3 list buckets
description: List all S3 buckets in your AWS account.

title: AWS S3 sync directories
description: Synchronize local directory with S3 bucket, uploading only changed files.

title: Find files by size
description: Find files larger than 100MB in specified directory tree.

title: Monitor system resources
description: Interactive process viewer with CPU, memory, and system load monitoring.

title: Network connections
description: Show all listening ports with process information (classic networking tool).

title: Disk usage by directory
description: Interactive disk usage analyzer with navigation and cleanup options.
